selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

messages.content:

Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion:
As a Buddhist, I've found that managing fake content requires patience and mindfulness. It's like observing the world without getting tangled in its illusions.

I spend only one hour each day on social media—enough to see what’s happening but not so much that it affects me. In this time, I notice how people post things online that aren’t real, whether they’re fake news stories or photoshopped images of perfection.

My depression makes these encounters especially challenging; seeing all the fakeness can be unsettling for someone like myself who is trying to stay grounded in reality through mindfulness practices.

But I also believe in compassion—maybe those people posting fake content are just as confused about reality as I sometimes feel. So when I encounter misinformation, rather than getting angry or frustrated (which my moderate conscientiousness and high extraversion actually make me prone to), I try not to judge them too harshly.

Instead, I look for ways to gently point out the inauthenticity without causing more harm—like sharing reliable sources from my education background. This approach feels true to Buddhist principles of kindness and wisdom.
